     The Numbers Business Report

The Man From U.N.C.L.E. (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $45,445,109Details
International Box Office $64,600,000
Worldwide Box Office $110,045,109
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $5,374,153
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,914,053
Total $9,288,206
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Set against the backdrop of the early 1960s, at the height of the Cold War, "The Man from U.N.C.L.E." centers on CIA agent Solo and KGB agent Kuryakin. Forced to put aside longstanding hostilities, the two team up on a joint mission to stop a mysterious international criminal organization, which is bent on destabilizing the fragile balance of power through the proliferation of nuclear weapons and technology. The duo's only lead is the daughter of a vanished German scientist, who is the key to infiltrating the criminal organization, and they must race against time to find him and prevent a worldwide catastrophe.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$13,421,036 (29.5% of total gross)
Legs:3.39 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:41.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$75,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,638 opening theaters/3,673 max. theaters, 4.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $64,474,913

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 14th, 2015 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
August 14th, 2015 (IMAX) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: August 12th, 2015 (Wide) (Thailand)
August 13th, 2015 (Wide) (New Zealand)
August 13th, 2015 (Wide) (Taiwan)
August 13th, 2015 (Wide) (Austria)
August 13th, 2015 (Wide) (Germany)
Video Release: October 27th, 2015 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for action violence, some suggestive content, and partial nudity.
(Rating bulletin 2341, 9/24/2014)
Running Time: 106 minutes
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Plot point: C.I.A., Commies, KGB, Nazis Outside of World War II, Terrorism
Social setting: Cold War
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Time period setting: 1960s
Source:Based on TV
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Guy Ritchie, Davis Entertainment, Wigram Productions, RatPac Entertainment, Dune Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Set against the backdrop of the early 1960s, at the height of the Cold War, "The Man from U.N.C.L.E." centers on CIA agent Solo and KGB agent Kuryakin. Forced to put aside longstanding hostilities, the two team up on a joint mission to stop a mysterious international criminal organization, which is bent on destabilizing the fragile balance of power through the proliferation of nuclear weapons and technology. The duo's only lead is the daughter of a vanished German scientist, who is the key to infiltrating the criminal organization, and they must race against time to find him and prevent a worldwide catastrophe.
